raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"I keep thinking that every time I look at my reflection in the studio, I see more flaws than talent, and that makes me doubt if I even belong here. The anxiety is so strong it almost feels like I'm just avoiding the hurt, and I worry that skipping the next session will just make me lonelier. I guess I'm stuck in this loop of feeling unworthy and just want to stop. I don't know if I can keep putting myself out there.","problems_referred":["body_image_concerns","general_anxiety","loneliness","low_self_esteem","procrastination"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: I keep thinking that every time I look at my reflection in the studio, I see more flaws than talent, and that makes me doubt if I even belong here. The anxiety is so strong it almost feels like I'm just avoiding the hurt, and I worry that skipping the next session will just make me lonelier. I guess I'm stuck in this loop of feeling unworthy and just want to stop. I don't know if I can keep putting myself out there.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here as a supportive friend, and you're expressing resistance to continuing with the painting class due to feelings of inadequacy and anxiety. Given your intent to resist, I'll use strategies like support and complex_reflection to acknowledge your emotions without arguing. The evidence shows you're struggling with self-doubt and feelings of unworthiness, which are deeply rooted in your core beliefs. Your recent message highlights the pain of feeling like you don't belong, and the anxiety that comes with it. My entry point will be to acknowledge the pain of seeing flaws in your reflection and how that affects your sense of belonging.

evidence_used.content: feeling like I'm not good enough
I am not worthy of connection or validation
anxiety keeps me from even showing up again

parsed.final_response: It's really tough to see yourself in a positive light when all you can focus on are the flaws, and it's even harder when it feels like those flaws are staring back at you from your reflection in the studio. It sounds like that's what's happening for you right now, and it's making you wonder if you even belong in the painting class. Yeah, it's a really painful feeling to think you're not good enough, and it's understandable that you'd want to avoid that hurt. Can you tell me more about what you think would need to happen for you to feel like you do belong, and that your art is worth sharing?
